coverage_criteria: Provides their current role or title, such as job title, academic position, or equivalent.
States how long they have been in their current role or position (in years, months, etc.).
Names a field, industry, or area (e.g., AI, healthcare, finance). A broad field name is sufficient — do NOT probe for sub-specialties, research focus, or sub-areas within the field.

subtopics.description: Breadth: the list of tasks the person performs in a typical week.
coverage_criteria: Names the distinct tasks they actually perform — specific actions and objects, NOT abstract categories like 'research' or 'admin'
Every task should have a single action and object, not a general category or responsibility.
Make sure to ask for a complete list of tasks, not just the most important ones.
Do NOT keep asking for specifics if the person has already listed their main activities. Confirm that the list is complete and move on.

subtopics.description: Time allocation: how much time they spend on each task, as a percentage of their total work week
coverage_criteria: The participant provides an approximate percentage of time they spend on each task.
The percentages add up to 100% of their total work week.

subtopics.description: Priority tasks: which tasks are most important, central, or impactful in their work
coverage_criteria: Names the tasks the participant considers highest priority or most central to their role.
Distinguishes priority from time-spent — a task can be low-frequency but high-stakes.
Name the tasks that the participant considers a bottleneck in their work.
